description: Field-level selective disclosure: per-field salted commitments, a canonical commitment vector, and a root digest (disclosure_ref). A discloser opens a subset of fields; a verifier confirms opened values against the vector and the vector against the published root, without ever seeing closed fields' values.

vectors.description: Open agent_id, currency, jurisdiction; amount, counterparty_id, settlement_ts stay closed. Opened fields recompute to their committed digests; the full commitment_vector recomputes to the published disclosure_ref -- unchanged by which fields were chosen for disclosure.

A closed field (amount) is tampered: its committed digest is replaced with the digest of a different (attacker-chosen) amount value, using the same salt. The commitment_vector's own recomputed root no longer matches the published disclosure_ref -- the tamper is caught structurally, without the field ever needing to be opened.
vectors.note: tampered_digest = field_digest('amount', 999999, salts.amount) -- attacker substitutes a different value's digest under the amount value the true salt would produce. amount's original digest was field_digest('amount', 48250, salts.amount).

vectors.description: Two related, structurally distinct attacks against the same closed field (amount): (a) salt reuse -- amount and counterparty_id are committed under the identical salt, collapsing both fields' effective dictionary-attack resistance to one; (b) digest substitution -- amount's stored commitment-vector entry is replaced verbatim with jurisdiction's digest (a digest that is valid, just for a different field). Both are detectable, by different mechanisms: (a) is a structural uniqueness check over the salts, independent of any hash recomputation; (b) breaks the root immediately (same class as neg-hidden-field-altered) and additionally fails on later disclosure because amount's field-name binding does not match what was actually committed.

salt_reuse.note: Structural conformance failure per docs/spec/disclosure-scoped-ref.md Verification step 3 -- caught by a uniqueness scan over the salt set, not by any root or field-digest recomputation. Root still hashes 'correctly' under the reused salt; the defect is the reduced dictionary-attack cost, not a broken chain.

digest_substitution.note: Root breaks immediately (same as neg-hidden-field-altered). Independently, if amount is later opened with its TRUE value (48250) and TRUE salt, field_digest('amount', 48250, salts.amount) does not equal the substituted stored digest -- the field-name binding inside jurisdiction's original commitment (field:'jurisdiction') means it can never validate as amount's commitment, confirming the substitution is caught on disclosure too, not only via the root.
